title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Recovery Case: Credential Transport
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git credential transport increment in a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Credential Transport
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local main is 2 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Credential Transport contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Credential Transport while producing a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local main is 2 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy. A defensible solution applies Credential Transport, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-credential-transport — Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
git remote -v
git fetch --prune origin
git log --left-right --graph --oneline main...origin/main
git push --dry-run origin main:main
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful SSH connection proves permission to push every branch. This fails the Credential Transport contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
